title: ConsumeRequest
description: Wire payload sent to POST /v2/permits/:id/consume. Closes the protect() lifecycle: evaluate → verifyPermit → callback → CONSUME. The raw payload is never sent — only its canonical hash — so the server can bind the proof to what the callback actually ran against without ever seeing customer data.

permit_id.description: The decision_id from the originating /v2/evaluate call.

payload_hash.description: SHA-256 hex of canonicalJSON(payload). Server cross-checks against the hash submitted on evaluate; mismatch = payload_hash_mismatch on verify.

execution_status.description: Outcome of the wrapped callback. On 'failed', the SDK still calls consume so the proof records the attempt — the original error is re-thrown to the caller unchanged.

execution_hash.description: Optional SHA-256 hex of execution-result metadata (e.g. deploy sha). Omit when the caller has nothing to bind. Open question: should we accept a free-form metadata object and hash it server-side? Recommendation: no — client-side hashing keeps payload bodies off the wire.

api_key.description: API key echoed in the body for wire parity with /v1-evaluate.
